layers.name: Silicon IP & EDA
layers.role: Where every chip starts: instruction-set licences and the design software no chipmaker can ship without. Royalty-like economics, agnostic to who wins the chip race.
layers.watch: Licence/royalty growth and AI-driven design starts; EDA pricing power as chip complexity explodes.

companies.name: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.
companies.note: the other half of the EDA duopoly

companies.name: Arm Holdings plc
companies.note: CPU architecture licensed across phones, servers, AI accelerators

companies.name: Synopsys, Inc.
companies.note: EDA design software + interface IP duopolist

layers.name: Accelerators & Logic
layers.role: The compute itself: GPUs, custom AI ASICs, and the CPUs that host them. The layer that re-rated first (2023-24) and still sets the tone for the whole chain.
layers.watch: Hyperscaler capex guides; custom-silicon (ASIC) share vs GPUs; inference vs training mix.

companies.name: Broadcom Inc.
companies.note: custom AI ASICs for hyperscalers + networking silicon

companies.name: Intel Corporation
companies.note: x86 host CPUs + foundry turnaround optionality

layers.name: Memory & Storage
layers.role: HBM feeds the GPUs; DRAM and NAND hold the data. The 2025-26 bottleneck: all three HBM makers pre-sold their capacity and the commodity cycle broke upward — the layer of the Micron trade.
layers.watch: HBM pricing and capacity adds; DRAM/NAND spot prices; data-centre share of bit demand (now >50% for the first time).

companies.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
companies.note: HBM + DRAM/NAND; the US memory pure-play

companies.name: Western Digital Corporation
companies.note: hard drives for AI data lakes

layers.name: Foundry & Manufacturing
layers.role: Whoever designs the chip, one of these makes it. Advanced packaging (CoWoS) capacity is as scarce as wafers.
layers.watch: TSMC capex + advanced-node/CoWoS capacity; 2026 revenue guided >30% on AI demand; US/Japan fab ramps.

layers.name: Equipment & Materials
layers.role: The picks-and-shovels sellers to every fab on earth: lithography, deposition, etch, test, and the ultra-pure materials in between.
layers.watch: WFE (wafer-fab equipment) spend forecasts; EUV shipments; HBM-driven test/packaging equipment orders.

companies.name: ASML Holding N.V. - New York Re

layers.name: Networking & Optics
layers.role: AI clusters are only as fast as the fabric between GPUs. Switching, retimers, and optical interconnects — a named candidate for the next bottleneck after memory.
layers.watch: 800G/1.6T optics ramps; scale-up vs scale-out fabric wins; co-packaged optics timelines.

layers.name: Servers, Power & Cooling
layers.role: Racks, integration, liquid cooling, and in-rack power. Every GPU ships inside someone's server with someone's cooling loop.
layers.watch: Vertiv backlog (+109% y/y) and NVIDIA power-architecture partnership; liquid-cooling attach rates; ODM margins.

companies.name: Vertiv Holdings, LLC
companies.note: power + liquid cooling; most direct buildout play

layers.name: Power Generation & Grid
layers.role: The hard constraint nobody can print more of: electrons. Turbines, grid equipment, nuclear PPAs, and merchant power for hyperscale load — the layer that re-rated through 2025-26.
layers.watch: GEV electrification orders (one 2026 quarter beat all of 2025); data-centre PPAs; ~$1.4T grid electrification need by 2030.

companies.name: GE Vernova Inc.
companies.note: turbines + grid equipment; AI power bellwether

layers.name: Data Centres & Neoclouds
layers.role: The landlords and the GPU-rental upstarts. REITs lease the buildings; neoclouds rent raw accelerator capacity by the hour.
layers.watch: Leasing pipelines and pricing; neocloud contract backlogs vs debt loads; capacity sold out years ahead?

companies.name: IREN LIMITED
companies.note: power-rich sites pivoted to AI compute

layers.name: Hyperscalers, Models & Apps
layers.role: Where the spending is supposed to pay off: clouds renting intelligence, and software charging for it. The Mag-7 capex (~$527B in FY26) funds every layer below.
layers.watch: AI revenue disclosure vs capex; agent/inference usage curves; whether application-layer margins hold.

companies.name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
companies.note: open models, ad-stack AI, giant capex

etfs.name: Global X AI Semiconductor & Qua
etfs.note: full compute stack: chips to power

etfs.name: First Trust NASDAQ Clean Edge S

catch_up.name: SamsungElec
catch_up.thesis: Memory & Storage is hot (heat 67) but 005930.KS lags the layer median by 60pp with growth intact — the rotation may not have reached it yet.
